channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Precision nutrition for the prevention and management of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - The rising disease burden of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) parallels the changing dietary landscape accompanying industrialization, underscoring the growing relevance of nutritional science in d...

item.title: Multi-omics-based machine learning model predicts response and guides treatment in Crohn's disease: a case study in nutritional therapy-response to editorial.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Editorial -

item.title: Dynamic changes in serum gelsolin combined with clinical parameters to construct a predictive model for mucosal healing in inflammatory bowel disease: A prospective cohort study.
item.description: Medicine - Observational study - Mucosal healing represents a critical therapeutic endpoint in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), yet reliable noninvasive predictive biomarkers remain limited. Serum gelsolin (GSN) has emerged as a ...

item.title: Association between iron status and inflammation markers with GDF15 and BMP6 in inflammatory bowel disease: a single center cross-sectional study.
item.description: BMC gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), including Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), are chronic disorders with relapsing and remitting disease courses. Several clinical indicators and bi...

item.title: Tetrameric assembly disruption impairs CXCL4 chaperon ability for DNA and type I interferon immune amplification.
item.description: Communications biology - Chemokine (C-X-C motif) ligand 4 (CXCL4), an antimicrobial chemokine with multiple effector functions, is a known biomarker in systemic sclerosis (SSc), but it also plays important roles in inflamm...

item.title: In vivo colonic epithelial cell editing attenuates intestinal inflammation in mice.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - The management of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains challenging, primarily due to the insufficient precision and efficacy of existing therapies. Consequently, there is an urgent need to deve...

item.title: Use Patterns of Novel Janus Kinase Inhibitors After the First Safety Alerts: A Nationwide Register-Based Time Series Analysis.
item.description: Pharmacoepidemiology and drug safety - Janus kinase inhibitors (JAKi) are medicines used to treat inflammatory rheumatic musculoskeletal diseases (IRMDs) and inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs). In Europe, JAKis received marketing author...

item.title: Small Molecules as Advanced Therapy for the Treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease, Particularly Regarding Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Internal medicine (Tokyo, Japan) - Small molecules used to treat inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) are orally administered therapies that offer a rapid onset without immunogenicity. Currently, three Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitors (tof...

item.title: The evolution of CAR therapies across oncology and autoimmunity: mechanistic insights and therapeutic innovations.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Review - Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR)-engineered cellular therapies have progressed from early proof of concept into highly programmable platforms capable of mediating potent cytotoxicity and precise imm...

item.title: The Longer-Term Impact of Biosimilar Switching Policies in Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease in British Columbia, Canada: A Retrospective, Population-Based Study.
item.description: Pharmacoepidemiology and drug safety - In 2019, British Columbia (BC) became the first jurisdiction in North America to introduce a mandatory non-medical switching (NMS) policy requiring patients with Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative...

item.title: Clinicopathological features and cancer-specific survival in young patients with ulcerative colitis-associated colorectal neoplasia: a nationwide cohort study in Japan.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The incidence of ulcerative colitis-associated colorectal neoplasia (UCAN) is increasing, but clinicopathological features and outcomes in young patients remain unclear. We aimed to clarify the cha...

item.title: Efficacy and safety of freshly collected autologous adipose tissue for complex anal fistulas in non-IBD patients: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Complex cryptoglandular anal fistulas present a treatment challenge, with many surgical options associated with recurrence, variable healing rates, and risk of incontinence. Freshly collected autol...

item.title: Intrahepatic Cholangiocarcinoma in Small-Duct Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis Associated With Crohn Disease: First Reported Case and Surveillance Implications.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Small-duct primary sclerosing cholangitis (sd-PSC) is traditionally regarded as a milder variant of PSC with a lower risk of hepatobiliary malignancy. Its association with intrahepatic cholangiocar...

item.title: Case Study of Fulminant Pyoderma Gangrenosum: Conservative Therapy-Cornerstone of Management.
item.description: International medical case reports journal - Case report - Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) is a rare, immune-mediated neutrophilic dermatosis characterized by painful, rapidly progressive ulceration and a propensity for pathergy. Diagnosis is clinical and often ...

item.title: Beyond the Graft: Optimizing Post-Transplant Care in Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Review - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic cholestatic liver disease characterized by fibro-inflammatory lesions of the biliary tree. In the absence of available, effective medical therapies...

item.title: Integrative genomic and metabolomic analyses identify novel associations between mQTLs and common diseases in Han Chinese.
item.description: Journal of biomedical research - While prior metabolomic genome-wide association studies (mGWASs) have identified numerous metabolite quantitative trait loci (mQTLs) in European populations, their characterization in East Asians-p...

item.title: Protein Homeostasis Screen Identifies CK2 Inhibition as a Driver of CARD9 Depletion.
item.description: ACS chemical biology - CARD9 is a genetically validated target for autoimmune disease, with human expression quantitative trait loci (eQTLs) linking elevated CARD9 expression to increased disease risk and lower CARD9 exp...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease and psoriasis as causal drivers of psychiatric disorders: A two-stage Mendelian randomization study grounded in the gut-brain-skin axis.
item.description: Journal of affective disorders - Psoriasis (PsO), inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and psychiatric disorders(PDs)often coexist clinically. We hypothesize the gut-brain-skin axis mediates their cross-system interactions, though ca...

item.title: Implementation and Effectiveness of an Enhanced Recovery Protocol for Children Undergoing Surgery: The ENRICH-US Stepped-Wedge Cluster-Randomized Trial.
item.description: JAMA surgery - Despite evidence that enhanced recovery protocols (ERPs) improve outcomes in adults undergoing surgery, adoption for pediatric populations has lagged. To assess the implementation and clinical effe...

item.title: Clinical characteristics and treatment pathways of ulcerative colitis patients with isolated proctitis in Europe and the United States.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Treatment guidance and real-world data assessing the management and burden of isolated proctitis (IP) are limited. We aimed to understand disease burden in ulcerative colitis (UC) patients with dif...
